content.full_text:
February 28, 1991

Fair Political Practices Commission
Legal Division
428 J Street, 8th Floor
Sacramento, CA 95814

Gentlemen:

I am asking your advice as to how this Court should handle campaign reporting obligations as a result of the following fact situation:

Proposition B, a bond issue to construct a new Youth Guidance Center, was placed on the November 1990 Ballot by the Board of Supervisors. The judges of the Superior Court supported this proposition. As Presiding Judge, I was contacted by representatives of those favoring the measure to place a paid argument in favor of Proposition B in the City and County's Voter Information Pamphlet. This was done and the cost of the paid argument was $513. Because the deadline for printing the pamphlet was imminent, I agreed that the cost of the argument could be paid out of the Judges' Flower Fund, which is used for social purposes, with the intention that the fund would be reimbursed by the supporters of the proposition.

Due to an oversight, reimbursement to the Flower Fund was not made as had been intended. The question of possible campaign reporting obligations did not come to our attention until a reconciliation of the Flower Fund was made earlier this month. The supporters of the measure do not have funds at this time to make the reimbursement.

Thank you for your assistance.

Very truly yours,

JOHN DEARMAN
Presiding Judge
